spots.name: Senso-ji Temple
spots.trivia: Tokyo's oldest Buddhist temple, founded in 645 CE. Its Kaminarimon gate is iconic.

spots.name: Meiji Shrine
spots.trivia: A Shinto shrine dedicated to Emperor Meiji and Empress Shoken, surrounded by a forested garden.

spots.name: Edo Castle Ruins
spots.trivia: Once the largest castle in the world. Now home to the Imperial Palace.

spots.name: Shinjuku Gyoen
spots.trivia: A sprawling national garden blending French formal, English landscape, and Japanese traditional styles.

spots.name: Nihonbashi Bridge
spots.trivia: The historic bridge marking 'kilometre zero' for all Japanese roads since the Edo period.

spots.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
spots.trivia: The outer market adjacent to the famous Tsukiji fish market, renowned for fresh sushi and street food.

spots.name: Yanaka Ginza
spots.trivia: A charming old-fashioned shopping street preserving the atmosphere of pre-war Tokyo shitamachi.

spots.name: Zojo-ji Temple
spots.trivia: A significant Buddhist temple with a dramatic backdrop of Tokyo Tower.

spots.name: Yasukuni Shrine
spots.trivia: A Shinto shrine memorializing Japan's war dead, surrounded by cherry blossom trees.

spots.name: Tokyo Station Marunouchi
spots.trivia: The iconic brick station facade, opened in 1914. A National Important Cultural Property.

spots.name: Koenji Shotengai
spots.trivia: A retro neighbourhood known for vintage shops, indie music, and the Awa Odori festival.

spots.name: Tsurugaoka Hachimangu
spots.trivia: Kamakura's most important Shinto shrine, dedicated to Hachiman, the god of archery and war.

spots.name: Rikugien Garden
spots.trivia: An Edo-period strolling garden representing 88 scenes from famous waka poetry.

spots.name: Akihabara Electric Town
spots.trivia: World-famous electronics and anime shopping district, a hub of otaku culture.

spots.name: Atago Shrine
spots.trivia: Atop Atago Hill, the highest natural hill in Tokyo, famed for its steep stone staircase.

spots.name: Ryogoku Kokugikan
spots.trivia: Japan's premier sumo arena, hosting three Grand Sumo Tournaments each year.

spots.name: Kanda Myojin Shrine
spots.trivia: A colourful Shinto shrine in the heart of Tokyo, celebrated for its Kanda Festival.

spots.name: Ameyoko Market
spots.trivia: A bustling open-air market near Ueno with hundreds of stalls selling food, clothing and goods.

spots.name: Koishikawa Botanical Garden
spots.trivia: Tokyo University's botanical garden, one of Japan's oldest, featuring majestic plum trees.

spots.name: Hamarikyu Gardens
spots.trivia: A beautiful garden beside Tokyo Bay with a tidal pond, formerly a Tokugawa clan hunting ground.
